$description: Verdant component vocabulary — the design system made consumable by agents: component names, prop schemas, composition rules, usage guidance, and each component's DataContract inlined. Verdant is a fictional demo scenario. Generated by agent-layer/gen-vocabulary.mjs from system/specs/ — do not edit.

composition.shape: a composition is one node or an array of nodes; a node is {name, props, children?}
composition.childrenRule: a node may carry at most one child, only when its vocabulary entry lists allowed children, and the child's name must be in that list
composition.chipRule: plant-card and care-task-row always render one status-chip derived from their own status prop; supply an explicit status-chip child only to override its label, and its value must equal the parent's status

type.description: care verb — leading word of the row label, capitalised

plantName.description: which plant — completes the row label, one line, truncates with ellipsis

status.description: task urgency — rendered by the status-chip child, drives the due/overdue states

checked.description: marked-done in this session, awaiting the log-care commit; defaults to false

care-task-row.usage: Component of the Verdant demo scenario (fictional product). One row of the "Today" list: a scheduled care action for one plant — check circle left, "Water Monstera" label centre, status-chip right. Use only in the today list between the stat-tiles and the primary-button; tapping anywhere on the row toggles `checked` (the primary-button commits the batch). One action per row — a plant needing water and fertiliser is two rows, not a compound row.

contract.title: CareTask
contract.description: One scheduled care action as the Verdant mock API returns it (fictional demo data). The shape care-task-row binds; plantId references a Plant (plant-card.contract.json) by id. Derived fields are baked against the scenario's fixed fictional today (the brief's head) — committed demo data stays coherent forever.

plantId.description: id of the plant this task belongs to

plantName.description: denormalised for display — the row renders without fetching the plant

done.description: already logged in the scenario's history; done tasks carry status "ok" and stay out of the Today list

status.description: derived from due vs the scenario's fixed fictional today

text.description: the scenario's fictionalNotice string, rendered verbatim — never paraphrased or summarized

demo-notice.usage: Component of the Verdant demo scenario (fictional product). This is honesty surface #1: the visible label telling a viewer that the scenario they're looking at — its company, users, plants, data — is invented for the demonstration. It renders whatever `fictionalNotice` string the active scenario's copy carries, nothing more. The composing page decides which scenario is active and where the notice sits in the layout; this component's only job is to display the string it's given, unedited.

name.description: plant display name, one line, truncates with ellipsis

species.description: latin name — muted secondary line, omitted entirely when absent

status.description: care status — rendered by the status-chip child, drives the due/overdue states

photoUrl.description: square thumbnail; when absent, a token-tinted monogram placeholder (first letter of name)

plant-card.usage: Component of the Verdant demo scenario (fictional product). The unit of the "My plants" list: one card per plant — thumbnail left, name + species stacked centre, status-chip right. Use in a vertical list under the screen-header; the whole card is one tap target navigating to the plant. Never nest a card in a card; never place more than one chip on it — the card summarises, the detail view elaborates.

contract.title: Plant
contract.description: One plant as the Verdant mock API returns it (fictional demo data). The card binds a subset (see the spec's Data binding); care-task-row references plants by id. Derived fields are baked against the scenario's fixed fictional today (the brief's head).

health.description: long-run condition — distinct from status, which is care urgency

status.description: worst status among the plant's not-done care tasks, derived against the fictional today

photoUrl.description: site-relative image path; optional — never present in the demo data (no image assets in fixtures), so the card's monogram placeholder is the exercised path

label.description: the action, verb-first, sentence case — "Log care", never "OK"

disabled.description: true while the action has nothing to commit (no rows checked); defaults to false

primary-button.usage: Component of the Verdant demo scenario (fictional product). The screen's one committing action: full-width accent fill at the bottom of the content — on the today screen, "Log care" commits the checked care-task-rows. One per screen, always last in reading order; anything less than the screen's primary action is not this component. Presentational: the composing screen owns what "commit" means and when `disabled` lifts.

title.description: screen name, centred, one line — the screen's h1

showBack.description: leading back affordance; omit on root screens like My plants

showSettings.description: trailing settings affordance; the today screen shows it

screen-header.usage: Component of the Verdant demo scenario (fictional product). The phone app bar: back affordance left, screen title centre, settings right — always the topmost element of a screen, exactly one per screen. Presentational: it renders what the composing screen passes, it fetches nothing and owns no state beyond scroll elevation. Keep the title short enough to never truncate ("My plants", not a sentence).

kind.description: which reading — selects the tile glyph

value.description: the reading, rendered as-is — the tile does no rounding or conversion

unit.description: display unit set beside the value, e.g. "%", "lx"

label.description: caption under the value naming the reading, e.g. "Moisture"

stat-tile.usage: Component of the Verdant demo scenario (fictional product). One reading, one tile: value + unit large, glyph and caption quiet. Use in a two-up grid between the plant-card and the "Today" list — moisture left, light right, always the pair (a lone tile reads as an error, not a design choice). The tile displays; it does not judge — no good/bad colouring, no thresholds (urgency is the status-chip's job, on cards and rows).

contract.title: Reading
contract.description: One plant reading as the Verdant mock API returns it (fictional demo data). The shape stat-tile binds — display values only, deliberately not a sensor API. plantId references a Plant (plant-card.contract.json) by id; the screen selects the featured plant's moisture + light pair.

plantId.description: id of the plant this reading belongs to

kind.description: which reading — selects the tile glyph

value.description: already scaled for display; the tile does no conversion

label.description: human name of the reading, e.g. "Moisture"

value.description: categorical care state — selects the visual variant

label.description: visible chip text, uppercase, one or two words — the state must read without colour

status-chip.usage: Component of the Verdant demo scenario (fictional product). The one categorical state signal of the system: a small pill naming a plant's care state. Always a child — of a plant-card or a care-task-row, trailing edge — never free-standing, never a tap target. One chip per parent (the parent summarises one state); if two states compete, the parent's data is wrong, not the chip.

contract.title: Status
contract.description: One care state as the Verdant mock API expresses it (fictional demo data). The shape status-chip binds; plant-card and care-task-row derive it from their own status field.

value.description: categorical care state — selects the chip variant

label.description: visible chip text, e.g. "OK", "DUE", "3 DAYS OVERDUE"
